Tickets

The main JS conference in the US

November 17 (hybrid in New York) &
November 20 (remote), 2025

  • 7
    hands-on workshops
  • 40
    insightful talks, case studies, core team updates
  • 700
    engineers meeting in New York
Step into Web Dev annual narrative. See JS stars speaking at the biggest US planetarium. Witness the trends, meet the architects of change, and become a part of JSNation.
Reserve a spot Stream page Watch live Get Multipass Attend remotely Reserve a spot Watch 2024 recordings Watch 2025 recordings
Join waitlist for cheapest tickets!
– Cambridge Dictionary
Nation — a large group of people who share the same language, traditions, and history

JSNation US is a two-day, two-track event dedicated to Web development. It's your entry into one of the largest engineering communities worldwide, valuing both expertise and meaningful connections.

This year we expect the authors and core teams:
  • Cursor
  • MCP
  • TC39
  • Vite
  • Chrome
  • Bun
  • Vue.js
  • Node.js
  • Solid.js
  • Nuxt

Want to know the rest? Follow us

November

Pro & Free Workshops

Workshops will be held before & after conference days.

November

Mon

17th

Conference Hybrid Day

Join us in NY or watch online. There will be lots of hybrid networking and inclusive interactivity.

November

Tue

18th

In-person Party

Join JS party at 19:00-23:00 ET!

November

Thu

20th

Conference Remote Day

Streaming of both tracks, speaker QnA's, discussion rooms!

Deep Dives

AI Assisted Coding

How AI is reshaping coding.
To be covered in:
  • Workshop: Prototyping Full-Stack Apps With Cursor
  • Talk: The Human 30%: Thriving as a Developer in the Age of AI Coding Assistants
  • Talk: What Happens When an AI Has Access to a Node.js Environment? Spoiler: Wild Things
  • Workshop at AI Coding Summit (remote): Advanced Claude Code Techniques: Agentic Engineering With Context Driven Development
Master this Deep Dive with Multipass

AI Engineering

What skills are needed to implement AI strategies and succeed as a developer in the AI era?
To be covered in:
  • Workshop: Build a MCP (Model Context Protocol) in Node.js
  • Talk: Lessons from Building Enterprise Remote MCP Server?
  • Talk: Let's Build Your Personal AI Agent in JavaScript
  • Talk: Generative Engine Optimization: How to Build AI-Discoverable Websites
  • Talk: Weaponizing LLMs to Hack JavaScript AI Applications

Growing to Senior / Tech Lead

Master the essential hard and soft skills needed to grow from a Software Engineer into a Tech Lead or Staff Engineer.
To be covered in:
  • Talk: Beyond Code: Crafting Effective Discussions to Further Technical Decision-Making
  • Talk at React Summit US (remote): 5 Tough Conversations Managers Need to Have
  • Talk at TechLead Conf (remote): How AI is Impacting Engineering Leadership
Master this Deep Dive with Multipass

Features

Speakers Presenting in the West's Largest Planetarium

Join us in the West’s largest Planetarium to explore the latest in modern JavaScript. Enjoy an interactive, one-of-a-kind conference experience in a truly unique setting.

Public ferry to the venue with the Manhattan view

Relax and network with fellow developers on a scenic ferry ride enjoying stunning Manhattan views.

The biggest JS party in the US

Get ready for the biggest JavaScript bash in the US! Celebrate the future of JavaScript technology and connect with the JS community. Connect with industry leaders or show off your skills with arcade games.

Within a leap of the Statue of Liberty

Let’s seize the moment and capture the beauty of the surroundings.

Fuel up your connections

Kick off your day with a proper NYC bagel, enjoy lunch with Liberty Park and Hudson skyline views — all while swapping ideas with fellow JS devs.

Line-up 2025

The AI-Native Software Engineer Addy Osmani
Never Worry About CORS Again Jarred Sumner
Keeping Up with The Fast and Furious Web Scott Tolinski
Beyond Signals: The Next Big Shift in Web Reactivity Ryan Carniato
Advanced Claude Code Techniques: Agentic Engineering With Context Driven Development Cole Medin
The State of Node.js 2025 Matteo Collina
Node.js + TypeScript: A Native Integration Years in the Making Marco Ippolito
Fast Track Design to Code With Figma MCP Riccardo Erra
Demystifying npm: What Actually Happens When You Install and Publish Karen Li
Beyond JavaScript: Unleashing the Power of WebAssembly for Faster Web Apps Nitin Gupta
The 2025 State of JavaScript Testing Daniel Afonso
What Happens When an AI Has Access to a Node.js Environment? Spoiler: Wild Things Alfonso Graziano
Get Set Up for Performance Testing With DevTools Nishu Goel
Generative Engine Optimization: How to Build AI-Discoverable Websites Simon Ouyang
Weaponizing LLMs to Hack JavaScript AI Applications Liran Tal
and more to be announced...

web development
Scott Tolinski
Co-host of Syntax.fm, USA
Talk: Keeping Up with The Fast and Furious Web

I own and make video tutorials teaching web development for Level Up Tutorials, releasing a new premium series every month.

I also co-host Syntax.fm with Wes Bos. Syntax is a popular, light hearted web development podcast that teaches full stack topics while staying fun.

I enjoy breaking also known as breakdancing, and have been dancing for over 15 years doing shows for professional NFL and NBA teams. I'm inspired by a hot cup of green tea, excellent music, and Shaw Bros. kung fu movies.

SolidJS Creator / Sentry
Full Info
artificial intelligence
Cole Medin
Dynamous LLC, USA
Talk: Advanced Claude Code Techniques: Agentic Engineering With Context Driven Development

Tech leader leveraging AI to solve real business problems. Built and scaled AI solutions across multiple ventures - from custom agent architectures to enterprise RAG systems and local AI deployments. My biggest passion is teaching others how to leverage AI effectively. I run a private AI community and a YouTube channel breaking down complex AI concepts into practical, hands-on guides. My content mostly covers building AI agents and leveraging AI coding assistants in ways that actually scale.

artificial intelligence
Riccardo Erra
Figma, UK
Talk: Fast Track Design to Code With Figma MCP

Riccardo Erra is a developer advocate at Figma. He joined Figma as their first developer advocate in EMEA. Before joining Figma he worked as a software engineer for 15 years with the last few years spent as a design system engineer. At Figma he's empowering engineers to make the most out of the various Figma's tools tailored to developers.

Full Info
tooling
Karen Li
GitHub, USA
Talk: Demystifying npm: What Actually Happens When You Install and Publish

Karen Li is a concert pianist turned elementary school teacher turned software engineer. She is currently a software engineer at GitHub's npm team. Aside from engineering work, Karen blends education and technology by sharing her experience with the community. Her motto, "I invest in brains" captures her journey from teaching to tech empowerment.

Full Info
performance
Nitin Gupta
Expedia, India
Talk: Beyond JavaScript: Unleashing the Power of WebAssembly for Faster Web Apps

Nitin is a dynamic Full Stack SDE 2 at Expedia Group, India, who thrives on crafting exceptional web experiences. Over the years, he’s worked his magic on projects for Marriott, H&M, Expedia, and more. While React is his go-to tool, his tech arsenal also includes Swift, Kotlin, Spring Boot, GraphQL, and a bunch of other developer superpowers. Known for his love of dev jokes and pun-packed humor, he’s the kind of colleague who keeps both the codebase and the conversations lively. When he’s not coding, Nitin is either jet-setting to new destinations, smashing shuttlecocks on the badminton court, or jamming out while learning the drums. A fun mix of tech wizardry and good vibes, he’s always up for an adventure—be it debugging or backpacking!

Full Info
testing
Daniel Afonso
PagerDuty, Portugal
Talk: The 2025 State of JavaScript Testing

Daniel Afonso is a Senior Developer Advocate at PagerDuty, SolidJS DX team member, Instructor at Egghead.io, and Author of State Management with React Query. Daniel has a full-stack background, having worked with different languages and frameworks on various projects from IoT to Fraud Detection. He is passionate about learning and teaching and has spoken at multiple conferences around the world about topics he loves. In his free time, when he's not learning new technologies or writing about them, he's probably reading comics or watching superhero movies and shows.

artificial intelligence
Simon Ouyang
YC-backed Startup (Early-Stage), Palo Alto, CA
Talk: Generative Engine Optimization: How to Build AI-Discoverable Websites

Simon Ouyang is a Full-Stack + AI Engineer focused on building production-ready React applications and reliable AI workflows. He has worked in early-stage, YC-backed startup environments delivering 0→1 systems across both frontend and backend. His independent research explores agentic workflows, RAG architectures, and evaluation-driven AI development. Simon speaks at React Summit US 2025 and JSNation US 2025, where he shares practical insights at the intersection of modern frontend engineering and trustworthy AI.

Full Info
security
Liran Tal
Snyk, Israel
Talk: Weaponizing LLMs to Hack JavaScript AI Applications

Liran Tal is a software developer, and a GitHub Star, world-recognized for his activism in open source communities and advancing web and Node.js security. He engages in security research through his work in the OpenJS Foundation and the Node.js ecosystem security working group, and further promotes open source supply chain security as an OWASP project lead. Liran is also a published author of Essential Node.js Security and O'Reilly's Serverless Security. At Snyk, he is leading the developer advocacy team and on a mission to empower developers with better dev-first security.

Full Info
web standards
Iwo Plaza
Software Mansion, Poland
Talk: TypeScript on the GPU – Bridging the Gap Between Realms

Iwo Plaza is a software engineer & artist, trying to incorporate art into every project he gets his hands on. He's passionate about graphics programming and softening the learning curve for future developers trying to get into it. Author of TypeGPU and numerous interactive examples and games built using WebGPU for Web and React Native. His combined experience with C++ game engines and TypeScript's type system allow him to lead the project in a unique direction.

artificial intelligence
Francesco Sciuti
Devmy, Italy
Talk: Eye Tracking & a11y on the Edge: Build an Eye Tracking With AI Running on Your Device!

CEO @Devmy, DevRel Expert @Codemotion, Google Developer Expert (GDE), Microsoft MVP, Google Certified Developer, LEGO Serious Play Facilitator, Projects Manager, Software Engineer, Speaker/Evangelist/Trainer, AWS UG Lead

Francesco is a Google Developer Expert, Google Certified Developer, Microsoft MVP ed AWS UG Leader and an accomplished programmer with several years of experience in enterprise programming , high skilled in web development (frontend and backend) and in team project management.

I’ve worked for a long time as a employee and then as a freelancer, but nowadays I has my own company: Devmy – Software Factory (former Acadevmy), founded with my friend Andrea. He was also Content Manager and DevRel of Codemotion, the largest tech community in Europe with more than 200,000 developers, as well as the largest IT conference in Italy and one of the most important in Europe.

I love reading comics (strictly Batman!), playing the drums, riding a motorbike, playing padel and spending a lot of time with his beloved family.

Her will to share knowledge is only matched by her will to keep growing and learning.

He is getting closer to finding the world's best sandwich.

accessibility
Karly Lamm
Aviron Software, USA
Talk: Accessible by Default: Building Apps That Everyone Can Use

Karly Lamm is a Senior Mobile and Full Stack Web Engineer, currently serving as the Director of Strategic Software Solutions at Aviron Software. She has helped startups scale their products from early MVPs to acquisition, including one later acquired by Walmart. Karly specializes in JavaScript technologies with deep experience in React, React Native, and Angular. She is a strong advocate for accessible and inclusive code, with a passion for building products that work for everyone. In her current role, she partners with startups to design scalable solutions, mentor engineering teams, and implement thoughtful UI and accessibility practices that align with real-world user needs.

case study
Faris Aziz
Smallpdf, Switzerland
Talk: From Fragile to Future-Proof: Solving Web Monetization Chaos with Payment Systems That Scale

Faris Aziz is a Staff Frontend Engineer specializing in React, Next.js, monetization systems, and resilient web architecture. He's led teams in early-stage startups and scaling companies, built career ladders from scratch, and shipped systems used by millions.

His work spans greenfield builds and legacy refactors across Fintech, SaaS, Fitness, and Connected TV, with companies like Smallpdf, Fiit, Discovery, GCN, and Navro. He focuses on building performant, user-centric applications with solid observability and maintainability.

Faris co-organizes ZurichJS, contributes to tools like Raycast, and spends time contemplating life's great questions, like why the build works on his machine but nowhere else.

case study
Aikerim Belispayeva
Memorial Healthcare System, USA
Talk: What Data Analysts Wish JavaScript Developers Knew: Lessons from Healthcare Finance

Aikerim Belispayeva is a Senior Analyst at Memorial Healthcare System, where she leads data-driven initiatives to improve hospital billing accuracy, detect improper payments, and modernize revenue cycle management. With a Master’s in Data Analytics and a strong background in SQL, Python, and EHR systems like EPIC, she works at the intersection of data science, healthcare finance, and regulatory compliance.

Aikerim brings a unique perspective to the tech world — blending deep industry expertise with hands-on analytics to advocate for smarter, more user-centric data tools. She is also a recognized speaker and award-winning advocate for using data to drive impact in public health systems.

soft skills
Allison McMillan
Tavlin Consulting, USA
Talk: Beyond Code: Crafting Effective Discussions to Further Technical Decision-Making

Allison McMillan is a fractional VP of Engineering and Leadership Consultant with over 25 years of design and facilitation experience for gatherings, covering everything from meetings and strategy planning sessions to offsites and conferences. She’s previously held leadership roles at QuotaPath, DEV, and GitHub. Allison has worn many hats in multiple industries as a community builder, managing director, and software engineer.

Allison started a podcast about being a parent in tech, Parent Driven Development, and is an internationally recognized keynote speaker. She is very involved in the Ruby community as a former board director and President of Ruby Central.

You can find more writing, speaking, and consulting information on her website: www.allisonmcmillan.com

DBOS, USA

Peter is a co-founder of DBOS, helping you build reliable software effortlessly. He loves databases and distributed systems a little too much, so he did a PhD in them, working with Matei Zaharia and Peter Bailis at Stanford.

authentication
Kim Maida
FusionAuth, USA
Talk: Backend-for-Frontend Auth: The Secure JS App Architecture

Kim is a Developer Relations and Developer Experience leader and strategist, JS engineer, and identity and security standards stickler. She enjoys teaching, mentoring, and learning from folks in the product technology space. Kim is the Senior Director of Developer Relations at FusionAuth and is a Google Developer Expert in Identity. She loves to travel, overland cross-country, design stickers, and craft miniatures and artisan keycaps for mechanical keyboards.

Full Info
web standards
Elian Van Cutsem
React Bricks, Belgium
Talk: Beyond Boring: Unlocking the Web’s Wild Side

Elian Van Cutsem or "ElianCodes" is a full-stack developer, creative technologist, and all-around internet troublemaker based in Belgium. He’s the CTO at Vulpo and works in Developer Relations at React Bricks, focusing on developer experience, documentation, and education. Previously, he was a Software Engineer and core maintainer at Astro.

Elian is also a co-organizer of several major JavaScript communities and events, including BeJS, React Paris, React Brussels, and React Africa.

Alongside his professional work, he creates provocative and experimental AI projects, designed to challenge our assumptions about ethics, automation, and human behavior. His work blends code and commentary, often in ways that are both hilarious and uncomfortably real.

Elian is known for his passion, puns, and offbeat sense of humor. His talks combine technical insight with a strong dose of creative chaos—guaranteed fun on stage.

Full Info
blockchain
Brian Whippo
Algorand Foundation, USA
Talk: A JS Dev's Guide to Not Dismissing Blockchain

Brian heads global developer relations at Algorand Foundation, where he helps devs learn, integrate, apply, and contribute to Algorand's open source blockchain technology to solve real world problems. Before joining the Foundation, Brian led global strategic technology transformation programs at Morgan Stanley, where he worked with commodities, exchange and OTC derivatives, Prime Brokerage, Brexit preparations, and digital assets over 15 years. When not on his laptop, he's probably cooking up something creative, literally or figuratively.

artificial intelligence
James O'Reilly
Google, USA
Talk: AI-powered JavaScript Development

James is a Developer Relations Engineer at Google, where he empowers developers with the transformative power of generative AI and Firebase Studio. Having worked in technology for what one colleague called "eons in Internet years," his career has been dedicated to improving the user experience. He was an engineering leader for the original HBO GO, led the teams for over a dozen of Discovery's "GO" streaming apps, and once upon a time a Flash evangelist. James is a passionate leader who excels at building and mentoring world-class engineering teams, always happy to "own the gap" between technology and user experience.

Google, Canada
Talk: AI-powered JavaScript Development

Alex Astrum is a passionate builder working in Developer Relations at Google’s Firebase, where he helps teams ship production-grade AI features with Genkit, Vertex AI, and Firebase Studio. He authored Firebase codelabs on Genkit and Gemini Extensions. He enjoys presenting live workshops and volunteering at hackathons. His current focus is around the transformational potential of AI in the app building space.

JS gala in the stars

JSNation US rocks the Western Hemisphere's largest planetarium! Over 500 devs will gather at the iconic 300,000-square-foot Liberty Science Center with Statue of Liberty views. A truly stellar JS experience awaits!
Liberty Science Center 222 Jersey City Blvd, Jersey City, NJ 07305, US

Convince your boss

Are you ready to skill up and network with fellow devs at JSNation, but your boss is not sure about it? It doesn’t take much to convince a manager or team lead and explain the advantages of our event.

We’ve prepared a summary of the most important information to help you achieve your goal. Head over to our dedicated page and share it with your boss.

Your chance to get a free remote full ticket

Tech Discussions

Join discussions focusing on specific technologies. Hang out with people who are on the same page.

Discussion rooms on November 17 will be held in a hybrid format while on November 20 fully in a remote format.

01

Fundamentals vs Frameworks: What Developers Need to Know When AI Knows Everything

Shivay Lamba, Ryan Carniato, Daniel Roe, Matheus Albuquerque, Brian Whippo, James O'Reilly, Alex Astrum, Max McCrea

Fundamentals vs Frameworks: What Developers Need to Know When AI Knows Everything
02

Backend/Fullstack Development With JavaScript

Liran Tal, Shivay Lamba, Daniel Roe

03

Future-Proofing JavaScript Careers: Surviving Market Downturns and AI Disruption

Sergey Berezhnoy, Ayodele Aransiola, Nishu Goel, Noam Zingboim, Benjamin Schachter

Future-Proofing JavaScript Careers: Surviving Market Downturns and AI Disruption
04

Architecting for the Edge: The Next Fullstack Frontier

🎉 JS PARTY

November 18 - Zeppelin Hall Beer Garden (88 Liberty View Dr., Jersey City, NJ 07302)

In this place, you’ll find all the options you’re looking for—whether you want to dive into lively board games or relax in a quieter space to meet new friends while sampling draft beers. We’ve got a fantastic space to make your after-party experience exactly how you want it!

🍻 🤝 Drinks, Food, Friends, Fun

Dive into sampling some of our incredible draft beers while meeting new friends.

🎳 ⚽️ Enjoy a selection of board games

Football table, Cornhole and many more!
